channel.title: Asides on Deuts Log
channel.description: Recent content in Asides on Deuts Log

item.title: @deuts@deuts.one 01KTT12YQDD3FGFWP39YFQCENC
item.description: I keep seeing these references to MCP server of the #Grist changelog, but where is the full MCP server documentation? Where’s the official one, at least? #GetGrist
item.content:encoded: I keep seeing these references to MCP server of the #Grist changelog, but where is the full MCP server documentation? Where’s the official one, at least? #GetGrist

item.title: @deuts@deuts.one 01KTRJAB5SX9F5DRTSQY2J5DJ4
item.description:
Claude Fable is not “slightly more expensive” — it’s a premium tier.

API pricing per 1M tokens:

Fable: $10 input / $50 output Opus: $5 input / $25 output Sonnet: $3 input / $15 output

So Fable is 2× Opus and about 3.33× Sonnet. For most coding/finance/reporting workflows, Sonnet still feels like the default; Opus for hard tasks; Fable only when it materially reduces turns or rework.
item.content:encoded:
Claude Fable is not “slightly more expensive” — it’s a premium tier.

API pricing per 1M tokens:

Fable: $10 input / $50 output Opus: $5 input / $25 output Sonnet: $3 input / $15 output

So Fable is 2× Opus and about 3.33× Sonnet. For most coding/finance/reporting workflows, Sonnet still feels like the default; Opus for hard tasks; Fable only when it materially reduces turns or rework.

item.title: Fireshare
item.description:
Fireshare

Self host your media and share with unique links.
item.content:encoded:
Fireshare

item.title: Why Does Hermes Use So Many More Tokens Than Claude Code? : r/hermesagent
item.description:
Why Does Hermes Use So Many More Tokens Than Claude Code? : r/hermesagent

Reddit is where millions of people gather for conversations about the things they care about, in over 100,000 subreddit communities.
item.content:encoded:
Why Does Hermes Use So Many More Tokens Than Claude Code? : r/hermesagent

item.title: LeafWiki
item.description:
LeafWiki

LeafWiki - Self-hosted wiki. Single Go binary, SQLite, Markdown on disk. No external database required.
item.content:encoded:
LeafWiki

LeafWiki - Self-hosted wiki. Single Go binary, SQLite, Markdown on disk. No external database required.

item.title: @deuts@deuts.one 01KT3CE0J0J8BH1PV6JHCHKCNV
item.description:
Jollibee just got demoted from the MSCI Standard Index to Small Cap. My portfolio got the same treatment. At least we’re consistent. 🍗📉

My average: ₱183. Current: ~₱134. That’s a ~27% discount I didn’t ask for.

MSCI effective date: June 1. So the index exclusionary is officially behind us. Now we wait for the chicken to fly again.
item.content:encoded:
Jollibee just got demoted from the MSCI Standard Index to Small Cap. My portfolio got the same treatment. At least we’re consistent. 🍗📉

My average: ₱183. Current: ~₱134. That’s a ~27% discount I didn’t ask for.

MSCI effective date: June 1. So the index exclusionary is officially behind us. Now we wait for the chicken to fly again.

item.title: @deuts@deuts.one 01KSXXZKTE3Q7DZKK6MTR73Y13
item.description:
🧡 The Orange Jumpsuit Committee is now in session.

Chiz, Joel, Marcoleta, Bato, Jinggoy — same energy as the Blue Ribbon hearings, just a different dress code and tighter security.

🤖 AI-generated image for satire. Not a real prison photo.

#OrangeJumpsuitCommittee #SenatePH
item.content:encoded:
🧡 The Orange Jumpsuit Committee is now in session.

Chiz, Joel, Marcoleta, Bato, Jinggoy — same energy as the Blue Ribbon hearings, just a different dress code and tighter security.

🤖 AI-generated image for satire. Not a real prison photo.

#OrangeJumpsuitCommittee #SenatePH

item.title: @deuts@deuts.one 01KSHTV727RMXBTVPXJNCTF1X1
item.description: In n8n, “Bearer Auth” and “Header Auth” are functionally identical if you set the header to Authorization: Bearer . Pick Bearer Auth when the server expects that header — it’s self-documenting. Pick Header Auth for non-standard headers like X-API-Key. That’s the only difference. #n8n #selfhosted
item.content:encoded: In n8n, “Bearer Auth” and “Header Auth” are functionally identical if you set the header to Authorization: Bearer . Pick Bearer Auth when the server expects that header — it’s self-documenting. Pick Header Auth for non-standard headers like X-API-Key. That’s the only difference. #n8n #selfhosted

item.title: Lumina FInance
item.description:
Lumina FInance

A feature rich, self hostable alternative for managing your personal finances
item.content:encoded:
Lumina FInance

A feature rich, self hostable alternative for managing your personal finances

item.title: FormTo
item.description:
FormTo

FormTo, a simple, self-hosted backend for HTML forms. Add one action attribute to your form and submissions land in your dashboard. Email, Telegram, Slack and webhook notifications. - lumizone/formto: FormTo, a simple, self-hosted backend for HTML forms. Add one action attribute to your form and submissions land in your dashboard. Email, Telegram, Slack and webhook notifications.
item.content:encoded:
FormTo

FormTo, a simple, self-hosted backend for HTML forms. Add one action attribute to your form and submissions land in your dashboard. Email, Telegram, Slack and webhook notifications. - lumizone/formto: FormTo, a simple, self-hosted backend for HTML forms. Add one action attribute to your form and submissions land in your dashboard. Email, Telegram, Slack and webhook notifications.
